title: Master Data Structures & Algorithms
description: Complete course path for Master Data Structures & Algorithms aggregated from SEED-IT.

modules.name: TIME-COMPLEXITY: Applying time complexity to DSA problems

submodules.name: Applying time complexity to DSA problems

modules.name: TIME-COMPLEXITY: Log and Square root time complexity

submodules.name: Log and Square root time complexity

modules.name: TIME-COMPLEXITY: Space Complexity
modules.description: Learn how to analyze the space complexity of any algorithm.

problems.name: Difference in Auxiliary and Input Space
Finding Space complexity for Nested Loops

Malvika is peculiar about color of balloons

modules.name: SEARCHING-SORTING-NEW: Searching
modules.description: Learn essential searching algorithms in this module.

modules.name: SEARCHING-SORTING-NEW: Sorting
modules.description: Discover fundamental sorting algorithms in this module to efficiently organize data structures and boost problem-solving skills.

modules.name: BINARY-SEARCH-NEW-LEARN: Introduction to binary search
modules.description: Learn Binary search step by step and review various implementations

problems.name: Time Complexity Analysis of Binary Search
Binary Search Functions in Programming Languages

modules.name: BINARY-SEARCH-NEW-LEARN: More Problems
modules.description: Solve some more problems to understand the applications of binary search algorithm.

problems.name: Elements less than or equal to Num
Upper Bound in a Sorted Array
Floor and ceil in a sorted array
First and last occurrence of a given number

modules.name: BINARY-SEARCH-NEW-PRACTICE: Binary Search
modules.description: Practice intermediate level problems using Binary Search

problems.name: Find minimum in a rotated sorted array
Find how many times the array have been rotated
Split Array with Minimum Largest Sum
Kth Element of Two Sorted Arrays
Find the repeating and missing number
Longest Consecutive Numbers in an Array
Print subarray with maximum subarray sum
Check if a string is a substring of another
Sum of Beauty of All Substrings

modules.name: LINKED-LISTS-NEW-LEARN: Linked Lists
modules.description: The Linked Lists module teaches you how to create and manipulate linked data structures. It shows you how to implement singly linked lists, perform insertion and deletion operations, and solve basic problems using linked lists.

problems.name: Linked List - Insertion at k-th position
Linked List - Insertion at k-th position

modules.name: LINKED-LISTS-NEW-LEARN: Circular and Doubly Linked Lists
modules.description: The Circular and Doubly Linked Lists module introduces you to advanced variations of linked lists. It demonstrates how to work with circular linked lists and doubly linked lists, perform various operations on them, and apply these concepts to solve problems like the Josephus problem and create a music player.

problems.name: Insertion at end in Circular Linked List

modules.name: LINKED-LISTS-NEW-PRACTICE: Linked List
modules.description: Practice educational problems on Linked lists

problems.name: Critical points in a Linked List
Find the Length of the Linked List
Find Middle Element of Linked List
Remove Nodes with a Given Key
Detect Start of Cycle in Linked List
Count loop length in Linked List
Insert and Delete in Doubly Linked List
Check if Linked List is Palindrome
Separate Even and Odd values in a linked list
Find Intersection of two linked lists
Sort a Linked List of 0s, 1s, and 2s
Find Next Smaller value in Linked List
Delete the Middle Node of a Linked List
Delete All Occurrences of a Key in a Doubly Linked
Find Pairs with Given Sum in Doubly Linked List

modules.name: STACKS-AND-QUEUES-NEW-LEARN: Stacks
modules.description: This module covers the fundamental concepts of stacks, including push, pop, peek, and isEmpty operations. It also teaches stack implementation using arrays and libraries, and includes practice problems on valid parentheses, expression evaluation, and finding next greater/smaller elements.

modules.name: STACKS-AND-QUEUES-NEW-LEARN: Queues
modules.description: This module introduces queue data structures, covering basic operations like enqueue, dequeue, isEmpty, and isFull. It also covers queue implementation, practice problems, and introduces deques (double-ended queues) along with their applications in solving algorithmic problems.

problems.name: Queue - Print Numbers with a Given Pattern

modules.name: STACKS-AND-QUEUES-NEW-PRACTICE: Stack and Queue
modules.description: Practice educational problems using Stacks and Queues

modules.name: HASHING: Introduction to Hashing
modules.description: Learn about hashing, where data is mapped to hash values using a hash function. Also, explore how collisions occur when different inputs produce the same hash value and how to handle them using techniques like chaining and open addressing.

problems.name: Frequency of each element in the array
Using Hashing to Optimize counting frequency

modules.name: HASHING: Implementing Hashing
modules.description: Learn how to use hashing in real world problems and explore in-built hashing data structures in different programming languages.

problems.name: Minimum time to collect all coins

Representation of binary trees as array
Finding indexes of the children of a node
Delete top element in a Heap

modules.name: GRAPHS-NEW: Connectivity and Cycles in Graphs
modules.description: Learn about the connected components and cycles in graphs.

problems.name: Time Complexity of Naive DSU implementation
Check if two elements are connected

modules.name: GRAPHS-ADVANCED: Topological Sorting
modules.description: Learn how to find Topological sort of a Directed Acyclic Graph

modules.name: RECURSION-NEW: Fundamentals of Recursion
modules.description: Learn the fundamentals of recursion

problems.name: Count All Subsequences with Sum K
Generate All Possible Pairs of Balanced Parenthese

modules.name: GREEDY-ALGORITHMS: Additional Applications of Greedy Algorithms

problems.name: Snakes, Mongooses and the Ultimate Election
Snakes, Mongooses and the Ultimate Election

modules.name: DYNAMIC-PROGRAMMING-NEW: Introducing Intuition for dynamic programming
modules.description: Take a problem - implement various approaches - and eventually learn how dynamic programming works!

problems.name: Maximum Sum of a Valid Subset

modules.name: DYNAMIC-PROGRAMMING-NEW: Practice problems
modules.description: Common problems in dynamic programming

modules.name: DYNAMIC-PROGRAMMING-ADVANCED: Types of dynamic programming problems
modules.description: Common problems and examples in dynamic programming

submodules.name: Maximum sum path in a 2D Grid

modules.name: DYNAMIC-PROGRAMMING-ADVANCED: Practice problems
modules.description: Practice problems based on advanced DP concepts

submodules.name: Bitwise Operators - 2 (NOT, LEFT and Right Shifts)

modules.name: BIT-MANIPULATION: Implementing Bitwise Operators
modules.description: Practice what you learnt so far by solving some problems

modules.name: NUMBER-THEORY: Prime Factorization
modules.description: Learn about dividing a number into its prime factors and how to do it.

problems.name: Mastering the Art of Prime Factorization

modules.name: NUMBER-THEORY: GCD and LCM
modules.description: GCD and LCM give us basic relationships between integers. What are they and how do we compute them?

modules.name: NUMBER-THEORY: Modular Arithmetic
modules.description: How do we work with numbers modulo another number?

modules.name: NUMBER-THEORY: Sieve of Eratosthenes
modules.description: Learn to find prime numbers very fast, and why it's useful

problems.name: Prime Factorization Method using Sieve O (log n)

modules.name: COMBINATORICS: Introduction to Combinatronics
modules.description: This module covers basic concepts such as the Rule of Sum, Rule of Product, modular arithmetic, set theory, permutations, combinations, and the Inclusion-Exclusion Principle.

problems.name: MCQ - City Pathways Combinatorics Challenge II

modules.name: COMBINATORICS: Conceptual Problems
modules.description: This module focuses on applying combinatorial concepts to solve more complex problems, including topics like permutation subsequences, distance coloring, and robot movements.
